params.text:
Module 1: Project Overview & Objectives

WHO SkinNTD AI Image Collection Initiative

Welcome to the SkinApp Training Course

This course will prepare you to:

  • Collect high-quality clinical images of leprosy and skin NTDs
  • Follow proper consent procedures
  • Maintain patient dignity and safeguarding
  • Use the electronic data collection system

Project Goal: Build a diverse dataset of clinical images to train AI systems for improved leprosy and skin NTD diagnosis in resource-limited settings.

The Challenge

  • Leprosy remains underdiagnosed in many regions
  • Specialist dermatologists are scarce
  • Early detection prevents disability
  • AI can assist primary health workers

The Solution

  • AI-powered diagnostic tools
  • Trained on diverse clinical images
  • Supports healthcare workers
  • Improves access to care

Your contribution makes this possible!

Key Responsibilities:

  1. Obtain informed consent - Ensure patients understand and voluntarily agree
  2. Capture quality images - Follow photography guidelines for diagnostic value
  3. Maintain confidentiality - Remove identifying information
  4. Submit through secure platform - Use authorized data collection tools
  5. Uphold safeguarding - Protect vulnerable patients

Remember: Patient welfare always comes first. Never compromise dignity for image quality.

Responsibility Chain:

Level 1: WHO - TLM International Agreement
↳ License and oversight

Level 2: TLM International - Partner Organization
↳ Formal partnership agreements

Level 3: Partner Organization - Clinician
↳ Training and authorization

Level 4: Clinician - Patient
↳ Consent and care relationship

You are part of Level 3-4, with direct responsibility to patients.

Knowledge Check

What is the PRIMARY goal of this project?

params.question: What is the PRIMARY goal of the WHO SkinNTD AI Image Collection Initiative?

answers.text: To create a commercial database of skin disease images
answers.tip: Incorrect. This is a non-commercial public health initiative.

answers.text: To train AI systems for improved leprosy and skin NTD diagnosis
answers.tip: Correct! The goal is to build AI diagnostic tools to help healthcare workers in resource-limited settings.
Incorrect. AI supports, not replaces, healthcare professionals.

answers.text: To collect data for pharmaceutical research
answers.tip: Incorrect. The focus is on diagnostic support, not drug development.
